California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 15, 19 91
Barbara Altbaun
4005 The Hill Road
Bonita, cA 92oo2
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. I-91-117
Dear I"Is . Altbaum:
This is in response to your letter regarding potential
conflicts of interesi under the Potiticat Rerorrn-elt (the "Act'r).1
Your fetter has asked a general question and therefore this
i"=p""=" is in the natur6 of inf6rnal assistance.2 rn addition,
we cannot answer a second question you posed reguestingr
interpretation of the chula Vista City Charter.s
OUESTION
Are you precluded from running for or serving as rnayor of
Chula Visla because of a community property interest in stock in
Ultronics, Inc., the managing general Partner of chula vista
Cable.
CONCLUS ION
Under the Act, you are not precluded from seeking or holding
office for any reason. You may however be precluded frorn
participating in decisj.ons in which you have a conflict of
interest under the Act.
1 co.r".nr"nt code sections 81ooo-91015. AlL statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
commission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations Section
18ooo, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (section
83114; Regulation 18329 (c) (3) . )
3 Th" Fair Potj.tical Practices Commission is lirnited to giving
advi.ce relating to the Act. Advising you about the provision of
the chula Vista City charter would be beyond the jurisdiction of
the FPPC. we suggest that you discuss the relevant city charter
provj-sion with your cJ,ty attorney or with private counsel.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 a (9f 6)322.5660


FiIe No. l-9t-LL7
Page 2
FACTS
You are considering entering the race for mayor of chula
vista. 
You and your husband ol^rn a substantial stock interest in
Ultronics, Inc., the managing general partner of chula vista
cabte, a cable television franchise holder for the City of Chula
vista. 
You are concerned about potential conflicts of interest
which you would face if elected to the office of mayor.
ANALYSI S
Secti,on 87100 prohibits a pub)'ic officiaL from making,
participating in making or using his or.her official position to
influente a governmental decision in which the official 
knows or
has reason to know he or she has a financial interest.
An official 
has a financial interest in a decision within the
rneaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable - that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
frorn its effect on the public generally, on the official 
or a
nember of his or her immediate farnily or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand doLlars ($r',000) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a cornnercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official- status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dol]ars ($250) or nore in value provided to,
received by or prornised to the public official
wi,thin 12 months prior to the tine when the
deci-sion is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, Partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of managenent -
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
t*o hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
otticiat 
wj-thin 12 months prior to the time r{hen
the decision is made.
section 87■03 (a)― (e)・

Page 3

oeneral partner of Chula Vista Cable. Presumably, your investment

Chula vista Cable.

oresenting any report, inalysis, or opinion relating to the

j.nfornation, ie ar" also sending you a.copy of Regulation 18700'1

decision.

:-it"f ihood that it will occur' certainty is not

if an effect is a rnere possibility' 
it is not

foreseeable. (In re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC ops' 198'
copy encl-osed. )
Because any decision to develop property which would have
cable available to future occupants.would either benefit or cause
detrimenttochulaVistaCable,itisreasonablyforeseeablethat
;;;h-; decision would have a financial effect on chula vista

you have j,ndicated to rne in a telephone conversation that
tfr.i" "t" two competing cable conpanies in your cornnunity; - there

financial efiect on chula Vista cabIe. whether the
.fi""t-"i-tte 
ae"i=ion is positive or negative is j-rrelevant for

ofdecisionscouldn"veamaterialfinancialeffectonchulavista
cabIe.
we first consider situations l'here the decisions in question
wouLd directlv utr""t-cn"ii 
vi=tt cable' In this case' Regulation
i;ror.i (".py encro=Ea1 requires Yo' t?. disqualify yourself from
;;;i;;ip;ii'tf 
i" tt" dlcision, unless the decision wifl have !e
iinanciir efiect on chula vista cable'


File No. A-9L-LL7
Page 4
A person or business entity is directly involved in a
decision before an offici.al's 
agency when that person or entity,
either personally or by an agent:
(1) Initiates the proceeding in $rhich the
decision will be made by filing an application,
cIaim, appeal, or sinilar request or;
(2) Is a named party in, or is the subject
of, the proceeding concerning the decision before
the official 
or the official's 
agency.
(3) A person or business entity is the
subject of a proceeding if a decision involves the
issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation
of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or
contract vith, the subject person or busj,ness
entity.
Regulatj-on \a702.1(b) , enphasis added.
Thus, if chula vista cable is applying or rnakj,ng a clain that
would require a decision by a governmental body in which you would
participate as mayor, you lrouId have to disqualify yourself from
that decision.
whether a decision will have an indirect material financial
effect on an econornic interest is determined according to the
standard set forth in Regulation 18702.2, a copy of which is
enc Iosed .
PubIic cenerallv
The Act does provide that an official 
may participate in a
governrnental decision lrhich e/ould foreseeably and naterially
affect his or her economic interests i.f the decision will affect
the official's 
economic interests substantially in the same manner
as it affects the public Aenerally or a significant segrrnent of the
public. 
(Sectj.on 87103; Regulation 18703, copy enclosed.) In
your case, it woul-d be unlikely that the effect of any decision on
Chula Vista Cable witl be substantially the same as its effect on
the rest of the city.
we cannot foretell whether you wiJ.l have rnany decisions from
which you would have to disqualify yourself because of the
conflict of interest provisions of the Act. whether there is a
disqua),ifying conflict of interest nust be deterrnined on a
dec is ion-by-decis ion basis, and the fact of having a substantial
interest in a cable franchise will not, of itself, 
disqualify you
serving as mayor if you are elected. Your interest in chula Vista
cable, may, however, require you to disqualify yourself from
decisions which would have a reasonably foreseeable material


File No. I-91--\l'7
Paqe 5
financial effect in the company, as discussed above. we suggest
that, if elected, you request advice regarding compliance with the
Act reqarding specific decisions that could have irnpact upon Chula
vista cable.
I trust the above answers your questions. If you need
further information, please feel free to contact me at
(9L61 322-59OL.
Sincerely,
Scott HaIlabrin
Acting General Cpunsel
By: Susan L.
counsel,
Bobrow
Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are you precluded from running for or serving as rnayor of
Chula Visla because of a community property interest in stock in
Ultronics, Inc., the managing general Partner of chula vista
Cable.

CONCLUSION: Under the Act, you are not precluded from seeking or holding
office for any reason. You may however be precluded frorn
participating in decisj.ons in which you have a conflict of
interest under the Act.
1 co.r".nr"nt code sections 81ooo-91015. AlL statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (section
83114; Regulation 18329 (c) (3) . )
3 Th" Fair Potj.tical Practices Commission is lirnited to giving
advi.ce relating to the Act. Advising you about the provision of
the chula Vista City charter would be beyond the jurisdiction of
the FPPC. we suggest that you discuss the relevant city charter
provj-sion with your cJ,ty attorney or with private counsel.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 a (9f 6)322.5660


FiIe No. l-9t-LL7
